topic: Someone finds an unsigned birthday card in their mailbox
raw_output:
[story 1]

The mail slot clattered as I pulled the thick envelope out. It was a card, folded neatly, the paper a pale cream color. There was no address, no name, just a single, slightly smudged inscription at the bottom: "Happy Birthday." My fingers traced the unfamiliar script, a strange stillness settling over me. I looked around the quiet living room, the afternoon sun casting long shadows across the floor. Who had sent this? I checked the box, the porch, even the cluttered entryway table. Nothing. A faint tremor ran up my arm as I reread the simple words, a knot tightening in my chest.

[story 2]

I picked up the card, the crisp edges feeling oddly significant in my hand. It was completely blank except for the single, handwritten greeting. My brow furrowed, my gaze darting from the card to the empty mailbox outside, then back again. I turned it over, searching for any hidden markings, any clue that might explain its sudden appearance. A slow, deliberate exhale escaped my lips. I walked over to the window, staring out at the ordinary street, trying to force a logical explanation into the silence that had descended upon me. It simply didn't fit.

[story 3]

The card lay on the kitchen counter, stark against the granite. I picked it up, turning it over and over, my thumb brushing over the unaddressed message. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly. I leaned back against the counter, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, my breathing becoming shallow. This felt… out of place. I scanned the surrounding area, the familiar shapes of my apartment suddenly seeming alien. I kept re-reading the single sentence, my eyes scanning the paper as if expecting a hidden message to reveal itself.
